title: 10 best herbal creams for foot odor

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil, a key ingredient in herbal tea tree creams, has antibacterial properties that help kill bacteria that cause foot odor and infection.

Deodorizing properties: The natural antimicrobial and antifungal properties of tea tree oil also help eliminate sweat and reduce moisture on the skin's surface, preventing bacterial growth and reducing foot odor.

Antifungal properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to be effective against fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor. It helps prevent fungal growth and alleviate symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with foot odor because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, terpinen-4-ol, disrupts the growth of odor-causing bacteria, such as Staphylococcus and E. coli, that thrive on the feet. By inhibiting these microorganisms, tea tree creams reduce the production of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) responsible for foot odor. This natural antifungal and antibacterial agent works to keep the feet fresh and odor-free, making it an effective solution for those struggling with foot odor. Regular use can lead to long-lasting results.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi causing foot odor.
Linalool: This terpene has antifungal properties that help combat the fungal growth responsible for foot odor, particularly in conditions like athlete's foot.

Cymene: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi contributing to foot odor, while also reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oil extracted from the leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia is used to combat foot odor due to its antimicrobial properties.
Stems: The essential oil from the stems of Melaleuca alternifolia is also used to create creams that help reduce foot odor, thanks to its antifungal properties.
Barks: Melaleuca alternifolia bark oil is used to create creams that help manage foot odor, as it has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh leaves from mature plants in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Infuse 20g of dried leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has cooled to room temperature.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold cream's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the bacterial load responsible for foot odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of pot marigold cream soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with fungal or bacterial infections that can cause foot odor.

Antifungal properties: Pot marigold's antifungal properties specifically target fungi like athlete's foot, which is a common cause of foot odor, helping to eliminate the underlying infection and reduce odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with foot odor because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and saponins, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. By reducing the microbial load, Calendula creams help to control the formation of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) responsible for foot odor. Regular use of Calendula creams can also soothe and soften the skin, promoting a healthy environment for foot odor prevention.
remedy_constituents: Neryl acetate: This sesquiterpene acts as a natural antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the bacterial growth responsible for foot odor.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpene has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to inhibit the growth of microorganisms that cause foot odor.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound exhibits strong antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, helping to reduce the bacterial and fungal growth that contributes to foot odor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the skin.
Leaves: They contain antimicrobial compounds that help reduce bacteria growth, which is often the cause of foot odor.
Seeds: They are rich in essential fatty acids and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and moisturize the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom, ideally in the morning.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks, turning occasionally.
Combine 10 grams of dried flowers with 20 grams of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has taken on the desired color and fragrance.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container, discard the solids and whip the oil with beeswax to create a smooth, stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Lemongrass contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which are common causes of foot odor, helping to reduce the bacterial load on the skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass creams can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with excessive sweating and foot odor.
Antifungal activity: Lemongrass has been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that contribute to foot odor. Additionally, lemongrass has a deodorizing effect, leaving a refreshing citrus scent that helps to mask any remaining foot odor, providing a sense of comfort and confidence.
remedy_constituents: Citrral (or Citral): This naturally occurring terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to foot odor.
Limonene: As a bioactive terpene, limonene has potent antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungi that thrive in warm, moist environments and contribute to foot odor.
Geraniol: This terpene has been shown to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, helping to reduce the microbial load on the skin and prevent the development of foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used due to their high content of citronella oil, which has natural deodorizing and antimicrobial properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus contain a high amount of essential oils, which help to reduce foot odor and provide a refreshing scent.
Barks: Barks of Cymbopogon citratus contain oils that help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, contributing to the reduction of foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 bunch of cymbopogon citratus leaves with a sharp knife to avoid bruising the leaves.
Steam distill 10g of the harvested leaves in a 500ml water bath for 30 minutes at 100°c.
Mix 200g of distilled water with 10g of the resulting essential oil in a clean container.
Add 400g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil to the mixture and heat it gently.
Stir the mixture until it thickens and then pour it into a clean container to set.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as aloin and barbaloin, help to kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and yeast that cause foot odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-carotene and vitamins C and E, reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the skin, which can contribute to foot odor.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin on the feet, reducing dryness and cracking that can create an ideal environment for bacteria and fungi to thrive, leading to foot odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with foot odor because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including foot odor. Aloe vera creams can help to reduce the bacteria that cause foot odor, while also soothing and moisturizing the skin. This results in a decrease in foot sweat and an elimination of unpleasant odors, leaving feet feeling fresh and clean. It's an effective and natural solution for foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin, thereby minimizing foot odor.
Salicylic acid: As a phenolic compound, it exfoliates the skin and unclogs pores, removing sweat, bacteria, and dead skin cells that contribute to foot odor.

Chlorogenic acid: This polyphenol compound has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce the growth of odor-causing microorganisms, ultimately minimizing foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is widely used due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties that help control foot odor-causing bacteria.
Gel from Leaves: This gel helps in reducing moisture and preventing bacterial growth, thereby reducing foot odor.
Aloe vera Extract from Leaves: This extract is often used for its ability to absorb moisture and kill bacteria that cause foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of aloe vera gel from the inner leaf of an aloe vera plant using a sharp knife.
Combine the extracted aloe vera gel with 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt.
Add 10g of beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated and smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 20 minutes before whipping with a hand mixer.
Transfer the whipped mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that lead to bad smells.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream can help soothe and reduce irritation caused by fungal or bacterial infections, which can contribute to foot odor.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties in the cream can help protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation that may exacerbate foot odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with foot odor because of its natural deodorizing properties. The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in the Eucalyptus globulus plant help to combat the bacteria that cause foot odor. These compounds, such as cineole and eucalyptol, work to eliminate the odor-causing microorganisms, leaving the feet feeling fresh and clean. Regular use of Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to reduce foot sweat and odor, promoting a healthier foot environment.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce sweat and bacterial growth on the skin, thereby minimizing the causes of foot odor.
Caffeic Acid: As a phenolic compound, caffeic acid exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for foot odor due to their deodorizing and antimicrobial properties.
Barks: Used to make creams for foot odor due to their astringent and antifungal properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and potency.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine 250g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 250ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 2 hours at 60°c to facilitate oil extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture and add 10% beeswax to the resulting oil to create a smooth and stable cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage cream's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor, reducing the spread of infection and eliminating bad smells.
Antifungal properties: The antifungal compounds in sage cream prevent fungal overgrowth, which can contribute to foot odor by breaking down sweat and producing unpleasant substances.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with athlete's foot, toe fungus, and other conditions that can cause foot odor, allowing for a more comfortable and odor-free environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with foot odor because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in Salvia officinalis, such as tannins and volatile oils, work to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. By reducing the microbial load, sage creams create an environment that is less conducive to odor-causing microorganisms, thereby helping to eliminate or reduce unpleasant foot smells. This natural approach can provide a fresh and odor-free solution for those struggling with foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that acts as an antimicrobial agent, effectively reducing the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.
Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid compound that exhibits potent antimicrobial and antifungal properties, helping to control the microorganisms responsible for foot odor.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has been shown to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the formation of foot odor-causing compounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and antifungal properties to help control foot odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
Roots: Utilized for their antioxidant properties to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of odor-causing microorganisms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is present.
Infuse the dried herbs in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Germander extracts have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor, such as Staphylococcus epidermidis and Candida albicans.

Deodorizing properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in germander cream help to neutralize and eliminate foul-smelling molecules produced by bacterial breakdown of sweat, thereby reducing foot odor.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander creams may help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with sweaty feet, which can contribute to the development of foot odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teucrium chamaedrys, also known as germander, creams helps with foot odor because it possesses antibacterial and antifungal properties, which effectively combat the underlying causes of foot odor. The cream's natural ingredients, such as flavonoids and diterpenes, work to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, thereby minimizing the unpleasant smell associated with sweaty feet. Regular application of germander creams can help keep feet fresh and odor-free all day long.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A diterpenic ester with antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin.
Diterpenoid esters: Compounds with potential antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can inhibit the proliferation of microorganisms contributing to foot odor.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from the teucrium chamaedrys plant at peak potency for maximum benefits.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves with 4 parts coconut oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the coconut oil with herbal goodness.
Strain the mixture and whip the remaining coconut oil into a rich and luxurious cream texture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial and fungal growth on the skin, reducing the source of foot odor.

The essential oils present in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin. By reducing the presence of these microorganisms, lavender creams create an environment that is less conducive to foot odor, providing a fresh and clean feeling. This makes it an effective natural remedy for foot hygiene.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin.
Linalyl acetate: Another terpene that exhibits antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections that contribute to foot odor.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to combat foot odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
Flowers: They are used because of their calming and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that causes foot odor.
Stems: They are used because of their antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and set them aside for later use.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes then strain.
Combine 1/2 cup of infused lavandula angustifolia liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 2 tablespoons of glycerin.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Cumin's antibacterial properties help to kill bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus, which is a common cause of foot odor, preventing its growth and spread.

Antifungal: The antifungal properties in cumin cream help to combat fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor by breaking down sweat and creating an ideal environment for bacterial growth.
Deodorizing: Cumin's natural deodorizing properties help to neutralize and eliminate odor-causing molecules, leaving the feet smelling fresh and clean.

The active compounds in cumin, such as cuminaldehyde and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi that thrive on sweaty feet. By creating an environment that is less conducive to the growth of these microorganisms, cumin creams can help reduce foot odor and leave feet feeling fresher and cleaner. remedy_constituents: Cuminumol: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.
Cumarin: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to foot odor.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause foot odor.

Leaves: Cuminum cyminum leaves contain compounds that help combat fungal infections, which can contribute to foot odor, and their essential oils have been used to create creams that promote a healthy foot environment.
remedy_recipe: Grind 3 tablespoons of cuminum cyminum seeds in a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes until well powdered.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powdered cuminum cyminum with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture in the double boiler for 5 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes light and fluffy for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help combat bacteria that cause foot odor by inhibiting their growth and reducing their ability to produce odorous compounds.
Deodorizing properties: Rosemary's volatile oils, particularly cineole and borneol, have natural deodorizing properties that can neutralize and eliminate unpleasant foot odors.

Antifungal properties: Rosemary's antifungal compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, help prevent the growth of fungi that contribute to foot odor by breaking down keratin proteins in skin cells and creating an ideal environment for odor-causing bacteria.

The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, thereby reducing foot odor. Rosmarinus officinalis extract's Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid compound has been shown to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of microorganisms that contribute to foot odor.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis flowers in 1l of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 200g of the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted.
Pour the hot mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herb fennel contains compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which are the primary causes of foot odor. When applied topically as a cream, these compounds help to reduce the number of microorganisms present on the skin's surface, thereby reducing the production of the compounds responsible for foot odor. This makes it an effective natural remedy for managing foot odor and promoting fresh, healthy feet.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Fennel's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections such as athlete's foot, which can cause foot odor by breaking down sweat and creating a perfect environment for bacteria growth.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling associated with foot irritation and inflammation, allowing the skin to breathe better and reducing the likelihood of moisture accumulation that contributes to foot odor.

Antibacterial: Fennel's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that thrive in warm, moist environments like between the toes or under toenails, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) responsible for foot odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing foot odor by preventing microbial growth.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a compound called anethole, which has natural deodorizing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to eliminate foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare seeds in bulk and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder for 10 seconds.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare seeds with 100 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes to create an infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any remaining solids and obtain a clear liquid extract containing 20% foeniculum vulgare content.
Mix the extract with 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, melting the ingredients at 70°c for 15 minutes until fully incorporated.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours before use as a natural foot cream to reduce foot odor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herb's active ingredients, such as geraniol and geranial, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungus and bacteria that cause unpleasant foot odors. Additionally, wild geranium has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with athlete's foot, fungal infections, and other conditions that can lead to foot odor. By using a herbal wild geranium cream on the feet, individuals can effectively combat foot odor and promote healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Wild geranium's antibacterial compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to combat bacteria that cause foot odor by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in wild geranium creams can reduce swelling and irritation caused by fungal or bacterial infections, which can contribute to unpleasant odors.

Antifungal properties: Wild geranium's antifungal compounds, such as phenolic acids and terpenoids, can help to prevent the growth of fungi that cause foot odor and athlete's foot, promoting a healthy and odor-free environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain triterpenoids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections causing foot odor.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids, which exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activity, making them effective in controlling the growth of microorganisms responsible for foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality geranium maculatum leaves in small quantities to ensure potency.
Combine one part dried geranium maculatum leaves with two parts sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids, then add 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets to thicken the mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a warm water bath until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, effectively combat the bacteria that cause foot odor, reducing sweat and moisture accumulation on the skin. This creates a healthy environment for the feet, preventing the growth of fungi and bacteria that can contribute to unpleasant odors. Regular use of herbal thyme creams can leave your feet feeling fresh and clean, eliminating embarrassing foot odor and promoting overall foot health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor, helping to eliminate the source of the smell.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness associated with fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor.
Antifungal properties: Thyme's antifungal compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause fungal infections, including those that can lead to foot odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their antimicrobial properties, which can effectively combat fungal and bacterial infections that cause foot odor.

Rhyzomes: The underground stems (rhyzomes) of thyme contain essential oils with antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them useful for treating foot infections and reducing sweat-related odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried thyme and rinse thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested thyme with 4 ounces of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax pellets with 2 teaspoons of coconut oil in a small heatproof bowl set aside to cool slightly before use.
Combine the cooled beeswax-coconut oil mixture with the thyme-infused oil and stir until fully incorporated then pour into sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The chamomile extract in these creams soothes and calms the skin, reducing moisture levels that can contribute to bacterial growth and unpleasant odors. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have antimicrobial activity, directly targeting and eliminating the bacteria responsible for foot odor. By applying a chamomile cream to the feet, individuals can effectively combat foot odor and enjoy fresher, healthier-feeling skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Chamomile cream contains flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against common foot fungi and bacteria, helping to prevent the growth of odor-causing microorganisms on the skin.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in chamomile cream can help reduce redness and swelling associated with fungal infections or irritation caused by sweating, making it a soothing treatment for foot odor-related discomfort.

Antifungal Properties: Chamomile's antifungal properties can help combat fungal infections such as athlete's foot, which is often responsible for foot odor, by inhibiting the growth of fungi and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating foot odor-causing bacteria and soothing irritated skin.
Barks: Chamomile barks contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with foot odor, while also providing a natural barrier against infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including 20 grams of dried chamomile flowers, 200 milliliters of distilled water, and 50 grams of beeswax pellets.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers with 100 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and let it steep for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible, taking about 5 to 7 minutes.
Melt the beeswax pellets in a double boiler with 100 milliliters of distilled water over low heat, stirring occasionally for approximately 15 to 20 minutes or until melted and smooth.
Combine the chamomile extract with the melted beeswax mixture, mixing well and pouring the resulting cream into glass jars for storage and later use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with foot odor because of its unique combination of natural ingredients that work together to combat fungal and bacterial growth. The antifungal properties in dandelion help to prevent the overgrowth of yeast and fungi on the skin, which can cause foot odor. Meanwhile, the antibacterial properties help to eliminate bacteria that can contribute to bad smells. As a result, dandelion creams create an environment that is less hospitable to microorganisms that cause foot odor, leaving feet feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties: Dandelion cream helps to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that can cause foot odor, preventing the growth of malodorous bacteria like E. coli and Staphylococcus aureus.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal compounds in dandelion cream inhibit the growth of fungal species like Candida albicans and Trichophyton mentagrophytes that can contribute to foot odor and athlete's foot.

Detoxifying and deodorizing properties: Dandelion's natural detoxification abilities help to remove toxins and metabolic byproducts from the skin, reducing the unpleasant odors associated with foot sweat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce fungal and bacterial growth, making it effective in treating foot odor.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits contain antioxidants and other nutrients that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers measuring 2 tablespoons.
Combine the dried herbs with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 10% of beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with foot odor because of its unique ability to neutralize the bacteria that cause bad smells. The cream's antifungal and antibacterial properties work together to inhibit the growth of these microorganisms, reducing the production of odorous compounds. Additionally, stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe irritated skin and reduce sweating, further minimizing the likelihood of foot odor. As a result, using stinging nettle creams on your feet can leave you feeling fresh and confident all day long.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream helps reduce inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to foot odor by reducing sweat gland function and decreasing the growth of bacteria that cause odor.
Antimicrobial properties: Nettle's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause foot odor, preventing the growth of odor-causing microorganisms on the skin.

Astringent properties: The astringent qualities of stinging nettle cream help dry out the skin, reducing moisture and creating an environment less conducive to bacterial growth and the production of unpleasant odors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated skin.
Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, which can also be caused by foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks in a warm dark place.
Grind 2 ounces of dried nettle leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered nettle with 6 ounces of cold-pressed coconut oil and 4 ounces of beeswax pellets, heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 180°f.
Remove from heat and let cool to 120°f before adding 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and stirring well for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store in an airtight container at room temperature for six months or refrigerate for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which effectively combat bacteria and fungi that cause odors. The cream's menthol content also provides a refreshing cooling sensation that helps to reduce sweat production, further minimizing the likelihood of foot odor. Additionally, wintergreen's antibacterial properties help to break down and eliminate any existing odor-causing compounds on the skin, leaving feet feeling fresh and clean.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has a natural decongestant and antiseptic effect, helping to reduce moisture and prevent bacterial growth that can cause foot odor.
Eucalyptus oil: Eucalyptus oil has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria and fungi that contribute to foot odor, while also providing relief from itching and irritation.
Camphor: Camphor helps to reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with fungal infections like athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and itching caused by foot odor.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the wintergreen plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various skin conditions, including foot odor, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and before midday sun intensity increases.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours or by air-drying it in a warm well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plant material with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Strain the cooled cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into sterilized jars, using 50g of the herbal cream per jar with a shelf life of 6 months when stored in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with foot odor because they harness the natural antibacterial and antifungal properties of echinacea, a key ingredient in these herbal remedies. The cream's ability to combat bacteria and fungi that thrive in warm, moist environments helps to eliminate the source of foot odor. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing properties can help to reduce friction and irritation between toes, promoting a healthy environment for feet that is less conducive to odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Kansas coneflower cream contains compounds like ursolic acid, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for foot odor, such as Staphylococcus epidermidis and Escherichia coli.

Antifungal properties: The cream's antifungal properties, particularly those of triterpenoid saponins, help to combat fungal infections like athlete's foot (tinea pedis), which can contribute to foot odor.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with skin conditions like eczema or psoriasis that can exacerbate foot odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, making them effective against bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.

Seeds: The seeds of the Kansas coneflower are rich in unsaturated fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry echinacea angustifolia flowers, leaves, and stems for infusion preparation.
Combine 200 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia herbs with 1000 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Mix 150 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating until fully melted.
Combine the infused echinacea angustifolia liquid with the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring until thickened to desired consistency after 30 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for foot odor are natural remedies that help combat excessive sweating and fungal growth on the feet, providing relief from embarrassing and uncomfortable foot odor. The best herbal creams for foot odor contain essential oils such as Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon nardus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Cinnamomum camphora, and Cinnamomum zeylanicum. If left untreated, foot odor can lead to social anxiety, avoid social situations, and negatively impact daily life and self-esteem.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to foot odor that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm, as well as skin conditions like acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem extract have antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal these conditions, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with foot odor is a blend of Tea Tree Oil, Aloe Vera, and Witch Hazel. Tea Tree Oil's antimicrobial properties combat fungal growth, while Aloe Vera soothes and moisturizes the skin. Witch Hazel, rich in tannins, helps to reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth. Applying a mixture of these herbal creams to the feet can help eliminate foot odor and promote healthy skin. Regular use can lead to long-term relief from foot odor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with foot odor because of its potent antimicrobial properties, derived from the Tea Tree plant. The active compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia, such as terpinen-4-ol, work to eliminate bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. By reducing the growth of these microorganisms, the cream helps to control the production of the compounds that contribute to foot odor. This results in fresher, cleaner-feeling feet, making it an effective solution for people who struggle with persistent foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): A natural antifungal agent that inhibits the growth of microorganisms responsible for foot odor.
Limonene: A volatile compound with antimicrobial properties that helps to reduce the population of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.
Caryophyllene: An antibacterial agent that helps to eliminate the bacteria that contribute to foot odor, thereby providing relief from the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are most commonly used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which is known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce foot odor.

Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia also contain tea tree oil, which is extracted and used to create creams and ointments that help to combat foot odor by killing bacteria and fungi that cause the odor.
remedy_recipe: Distill 100 grams of melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 1 liter of distilled water for 2 hours at 100°c.
Combine the distilled extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of coconut oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with foot odor because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The essential oils present in the cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi that thrive in warm, moist environments like the feet. By reducing the microbial load, Lavandula angustifolia creams create an environment that is less conducive to the production of foot odor, leaving the skin feeling fresh and clean. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief from foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with foot odor by its antibacterial properties, reducing the growth of odor-causing microorganisms on the skin.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate contributes to foot odor control by its antifungal and antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the spread of fungi and bacteria.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene helps with foot odor by its potent antimicrobial activity, preventing the proliferation of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their calming and antifungal properties, which help to reduce foot odor.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to dry out and kill bacteria that cause foot odor.
Stems: The stems are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of lavandula angustifolia flowers at dawn when they are fully open and dry them in the shade for 2 days.
Steep 50g of the dried flowers in 200g of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Mix 100g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the waxes melt and then pour it into a container to cool and set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with foot odor because of its potent decongestant and antibacterial properties. The oil extracted from Eucalyptus globulus leaves contains compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to reduce sweat and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor. By applying the cream to the feet, it can help to dry out the skin and reduce the moisture that contributes to foot odor, leaving the feet feeling fresh and odor-free for a longer period.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Cineole helps to reduce foot odor by its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause unpleasant smells.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide has antifungal properties, which help to control the growth of fungi that contribute to foot odor.
Eugenol: Eugenol has antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to prevent the growth of microorganisms that cause foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high menthol and eucalyptol content, which helps to reduce sweat and eliminate foot odor-causing bacteria.
Barks: Utilized for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the skin from infections and irritation.
Stems: Employed for its ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, helping to alleviate foot odor and related skin conditions.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of eucalyptus globulus leaves and chop them into small pieces for infusion.
Heat 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes at 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Steep the chopped eucalyptus globulus leaves in the heated carrier oil for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil with the infused oil for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in lemongrass oil, such as citral and geraniol, effectively combat the bacteria that cause foot odor. These compounds work by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, thereby preventing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that contribute to foot odor. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus creams can lead to fresher, drier feet and a reduced risk of fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This compound helps with foot odor by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause bad odor, such as Brevibacterium and Staphylococcus.
Geraniol: Geraniol has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the number of bacteria on the skin, which in turn decreases the production of foot odor-causing compounds.
Limonene: Limonene has a deodorizing effect and can help neutralize the unpleasant smells produced by bacteria on the skin, thereby reducing foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high citral content, which provides natural antifungal and antibacterial properties to combat foot odor.
Rhyzomes: Sourced for their citronella oil, which has deodorizing properties and helps to reduce foot sweat and odor.
Leaves: Also used for their lemon-scented essential oil, which has antiseptic and deodorizing properties to freshen and protect feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves from cymbopogon citratus at dawn for optimal oil content and fragrance.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 25-30°c for 7-10 days to remove excess moisture.
Steep 50g of dried leaves in 500ml of coconut oil at 70-80°c for 4-6 hours to extract oils.
Filter the oil mixture through a coffee filter and press the solids to extract remaining oils.
Combine 100g of the extracted oil with 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant has been shown to inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the occurrence of foot odor. Additionally, Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, creating an environment that is less conducive to the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to foot odor. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief and freshness.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce foot odor by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause unpleasant smells.
Amino acids (especially alanine): They aid in reducing foot odor by breaking down and neutralizing the compounds responsible for the smell, such as indole and skatole.
Volatile compounds (including linalool and geraniol): They help control foot odor by possessing antimicrobial properties, which prevent the proliferation of odor-causing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves is widely used due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat foot odor-causing bacteria.
Leaves: The leaves also contain aloin, a compound that has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce foot odor and inflammation.
Leaves: The gel from the leaves is also known for its moisturizing properties, which can help to keep the feet hydrated and reduce the risk of foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 10-15 fresh leaves of aloe barbadensis from a pesticide-free area.
Grate 4-5 leaves using a grater or blender to extract the gel.
Mix 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler at 150-200°f.
Add 2 tablespoons of aloe gel to the mixture and stir until the beeswax dissolves completely.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon nardus creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredient, citronella oil, inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor. Additionally, its deodorizing properties help to eliminate sweat and moisture that can contribute to foot odor. By applying Cymbopogon nardus creams to the feet, individuals can reduce foot odor and maintain fresh, clean feet. This makes it an effective solution for people who experience persistent foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Cineol: Helps to reduce foot odor by its antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause unpleasant smells.
Limonene: Contributes to the reduction of foot odor by its antifungal properties, preventing the growth of fungi that cause foot infections and odors.
Beta-caryophyllene: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to control the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high citral content, a key ingredient in fighting foot odor-causing bacteria.
Buds: Utilized for their potent essential oils, which help to eliminate foot odor-causing microorganisms.
Rhyzomes: Employed for their extracts, which possess antimicrobial properties to combat foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried cymbopogon nardus essential oil, 200g of aloe vera gel and 100g of coconut oil from suppliers.
Combine 200g of distilled water and 2g of vitamin e oil in a bowl and stir well.
Mix the cymbopogon nardus essential oil, aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a separate bowl for 5 minutes.
Blend the mixture from step 3 with the water and vitamin e oil mixture from step 2 for 2 minutes.
Pour the blended mixture into a container and refrigerate for 30 minutes before applying to skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause the smell. The thymol in thyme oil has been shown to effectively combat odor-causing microorganisms, such as Staphylococcus and E. coli. Additionally, thyme's antifungal properties help to reduce the presence of fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, which can contribute to foot odor. This makes thymus vulgaris creams an effective natural remedy for foot odor.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol exhibits strong antimicrobial activity, effectively inhibiting the microbial growth responsible for foot odor.
Linalool: Linalool has antifungal properties, which help to combat fungal infections that contribute to foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are primarily used due to their high content of thymol, a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent that helps combat foot odor-causing bacteria.
Roots: The roots of Thymus vulgaris are used for their rich essential oil content, which includes thymol and other compounds that provide antimicrobial properties and help reduce foot odor.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used for their fragrance and essential oil content, which contributes to the overall antimicrobial and deodorizing properties of creams for foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves in 2 liters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.
Combine 150g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Gradually add 150ml of the thymus vulgaris infused water to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with foot odor because of the natural antifungal and antibacterial properties found in the ginger root. The active compounds in Zingiber officinale, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi that thrive in warm, moist environments like the feet. By applying these creams, individuals can reduce the occurrence of foot odor and promote a fresher, healthier foot environment. Regular use can also help prevent fungal and bacterial infections.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.
Zingiberene: It has antifungal properties, which can help to control the development of fungi that contribute to foot odor.
Shogaols: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of foot odor by minimizing oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they are the part of the plant that contains the highest concentration of the active compound, gingerol, which has natural anti-fungal and antibacterial properties that help combat foot odor.
Roots: Roots are used because they also contain gingerol, which helps reduce foot odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
Buds: Buds are not as commonly used but can be used as an alternative due to their high concentration of gingerol as well.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100 grams of dried zingiber officinale in 1 liter of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the infused liquid and 300 grams of distilled water with the melted beeswax mixture in a bowl.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and becomes creamy in texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora creams helps with foot odor because of its unique properties. The camphor content in these creams has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause foot odor. Additionally, camphor is known to have a deodorizing effect, reducing sweat and moisture that contribute to foot odor. By using Cinnamomum camphora creams, individuals can experience a reduction in foot odor, promoting healthier and fresher feet.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: They have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause foot odor.
Phenolics (Cinnamaldehyde): They have antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi, which are often responsible for foot odor, and also possess deodorizing properties.
Limonene: It has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that help eliminate odor-causing bacteria and fungi from the skin, reducing foot odor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their camphor content, which helps to reduce foot odor by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause it.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their high camphor content, which helps to eliminate foot odor by killing bacteria and fungi that cause it.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml distilled water with 20g of cinnamomum camphora bark in a glass bowl for 2 hours.
Strain the water through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine 150g of coconut oil and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler to melt.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of lanolin to the melted oil mixture and stir well.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean glass jar and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams helps with foot odor because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, inhibit the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi that thrive in warm and moist environments, like the feet. This cream helps to create an environment that is less conducive to bacterial growth, thereby reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds responsible for foot odor. Regular application can lead to fresher and healthier feet.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing the growth of bacteria that cause foot odor.
Eugenol: Eugenol has antifungal properties, helping to combat fungal infections that contribute to foot odor.
Linalool: Linalool has a drying effect on the skin, reducing moisture levels that can contribute to the growth of odor-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used in foot creams due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce foot odor.
Leaves: Used in foot creams for its ability to soothe and calm the skin, while also providing antifungal and antibacterial benefits.
Rhyzomes: Used in foot creams for its ability to reduce inflammation and provide antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat foot odor.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 200g of dried cinnamomum zeylanicum in 1l of carrier oil at 70c for 2 hours then strain.
Combine 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter in a double boiler at 60c.
Mix the infused cinnamomum zeylanicum oil with 200g of distilled water and 20g of glycerin.
Gradually add the beeswax and shea butter mixture to the infused oil mixture at 50c.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes then whip.
